title: Mustardy Fluffy Filipino Pork Sisig
description: A twist on the classic Filipino dish, this fluffy sisig is bursting with flavor and features a tangy mustardy kick.

ingredients.name: pork face (such as pork jowl or pork ear)

instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the pork cubes and cook until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Mince the garlic and green chili and add to the skillet, cooking for an additional minute.
Add the mustard powder and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the fish sauce, vinegar, black pepper, and salt, and stir to combine.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Taste and adjust seasoning as needed.
Transfer the mixture to a serving platter and garnish with chopped cilantro.
Use a leaf wrapper to scoop up the sisig and serve immediately.
To assemble, place a small scoop of the sisig mixture onto the center of the leaf wrapper, followed by a sprinkle of chopped cilantro, and then fold the wrapper to create a neat package.
Steam the packages for 2 minutes to warm the sisig and soften the wrapper.
